behavior: Intelligent, often wary woodland corvid that forages in trees and on the ground. Omnivorous, with a strong habit of collecting and burying acorns; also eats other seeds, fruit, invertebrates, eggs, and small vertebrates. Usually resident but may disperse when food crops fail. Gives harsh, rasping alarm calls and readily mimics other sounds.
field_marks: Stocky, medium-sized corvid with broad, rounded wings and a long black tail
Variable crown, often pale with black streaking, above a bold black moustache stripe
Bright azure-blue shoulder panel finely barred with black
Strong black-and-white wing pattern and a conspicuous white rump
Stout black bill, pale grayish eye, and sturdy pinkish-brown legs
habitat: Woodland and forest with dense tree and shrub cover, especially oak-rich mixed or deciduous woods; also coniferous forest, wooded parks, orchards, and large gardens. Usually avoids extensive open ground.

sources.title: Birds of the World — Eurasian Jay: Plumages, Molts, and Structure
